purpose: F6 phi-vs-Re friction closure, Ri per segment, insulation caution, minor loss study, T13 proposal, geometry reference
scope: Salt 2/3/4 Jin mainline (3 cases); 4 corner bends; 5 major spans; TAMU natural-circulation loop

key_findings.F6_implementation: phi(Re, leg_class) = a×Re^b for heater (a=2.05,b=-0.027) and cooler (a=1.92,b=-0.050); constant phi=1.811 for downcomer (Re trend is Ri-driven anomaly). 72/72 tests pass.
key_findings.Ri_per_segment: Added compute_segment_ri() to solver.py; segment_ri Dict added to ModelResult. Ri = g×beta×|T_wall-T_bulk|×D/V². Self-consistent: no CFD values needed.

open_questions_for_user: MinorLosses K values in solver.py: do they match the CFD bend_minor_loss CSV K values? If yes, F6 phi should be revised downward by ~10% to use phi_pipe_only instead of phi_original.
T13 campaign: confirm high Q values (up to 5000 W, 17x nominal S3) are acceptable for CFD study of upcomer onset. Physical loop cannot reach these, but CFD can.
Geometry reference: should reference/geometry_reference.md be linked from CLAUDE.md Section 6 as the canonical geometry source?
